title: Amsterdam’s QDNL Participations Rebrands as Ground State Ventures, Secures Over €75.2M for Early-Stage Quantum Fund - Quantum Computing Report
gist: Dutch quantum VC QDNL Participations rebranded as Ground State Ventures and oversubscribed its debut early-stage quantum fund at over €75.2M ($88M), adding Rigetti founder Chad Rigetti as partner and pivoting from Netherlands seed deals to cross-border Europe/US checks, reporting a $1.3B portfolio valuation within 15 months.

insights.claim: QDNL Participations rebranded as Ground State Ventures and raised over €75.2 million ($88 million) for its inaugural early-stage quantum fund, oversubscribing its original €59.8 million ($70 million) target.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: An oversubscribed dedicated quantum fund signals renewed LP appetite for early-stage quantum despite long timelines; a fresh source of seed capital for the ecosystem.
insights.quote: The vehicle has raised over €75.2 million ($88 million), significantly outpacing its original capital target of €59.8 million ($70 million).

insights.claim: The firm is shifting from localized Netherlands seed investing to cross-border deal execution across Europe and the US, with operational bases in Amsterdam, London, and San Francisco.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: A regional seed fund going transatlantic increases competition for early quantum deals in the US and broadens its deal aperture.
insights.locator: The operational restructure and expanded capital allocation plan

insights.claim: The fund reports the cumulative valuation of its portfolio holdings has climbed to $1.3 billion within 15 months of deployment, backed by first-check positioning.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: A self-reported 15-month markup to $1.3B is a headline TVPI signal but is unrealized paper value; treat as a fundraising metric, not liquidity.
insights.quote: the cumulative valuation of its portfolio holdings has climbed to $1.3 billion within 15 months of deployment
insights.locator: Backed by first-check positioning in its assets

insights.claim: Founded in 2022 by GP Ton van 't Noordende as a pre-seed anchor for the Dutch quantum ecosystem, its early portfolio included Delft/Leiden spin-outs QuantWare, Qblox, QphoX, QT Sense, and Q*Bird.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: Maps the Dutch quantum hardware supply chain in one investor's book; several of these names also appear in the QuTech Tuna-17 consortium, showing ecosystem concentration.
insights.locator: Originally established in 2022 by General Partner

insights.claim: The new international thesis targets Swiss cryogenic microelectronics group Rhonexum and US entities Diffraqtion, Quantum Elements, and SiC Systems.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: A watchlist of the fund's newest bets; cryogenic microelectronics and SiC point to picks-and-shovels infrastructure rather than full-stack computers.
insights.locator: The new international deployment thesis shifts capital

insights.claim: The fund supported Rigetti founder Chad Rigetti's new venture Sygaldry Technologies across both Seed and Series A of a €118.9 million ($139 million) total round to build quantum-accelerated servers for high-density AI workloads.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: A $139M raise to fuse quantum acceleration with AI-server workloads is a large early bet at the quantum-AI intersection; Chad Rigetti's involvement as both founder and now fund partner is a notable network signal.
insights.quote: participating across both the Seed and Series A phases of the startup’s massive €118.9 million ($139 million) total capitalization round

insights.claim: Chad Rigetti, founder and former CEO of Rigetti Computing, joined as partner, and the investment team includes physics PhDs from Oxford, Harvard, Stanford, Yale, and UC Berkeley for hardware and protocol-layer diligence.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: Deep technical bench plus a marquee founder-operator strengthens the fund's ability to source and vet hard-tech deals, a differentiator versus generalist VCs.
insights.locator: The structural scaling of Ground State Ventures is reinforced
